content: Python Arcade uses OpenGL/GPU acceleration for sprite drawing (2-3x faster for sprites, transforms), has built-in physics and spatial hashing, encourages logic/display separation. Pygame is raster/CPU-based, very fast for pixel manipulation and O(n) rect-based collision (compiled in C), simpler mental model, far larger community/examples/StackOverflow base for immediate-mode line/circle/text drawing typical of radar displays. For an ATC radar display (thin vector lines, circles, text, dots at 60fps with modest object counts ~10s of aircraft) pygame/pygame-ce's simplicity and 2D-drawing-primitive ergonomics (draw.line, draw.circle, draw.aaline) outweigh arcade's GPU sprite advantages, which matter more for hundreds/thousands of sprites.
